title: 🎉 Budgety is now available for Monday.com
excerpt:
Hello everyone!:waving_hand:

posts.author: HuwenArnone (Huwen Arnone)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everyone!

We’re thrilled to announce that Budgety is now also part of the Monday.com ecosystem! If you’re already managing your projects with Monday.com, now you can also control your costs, budgets, and forecasts without leaving your boards.

What’s Budgety?
Budgety for Monday.com is a new budget management app that allows you to track budgets, costs, and expenses, allowing the opportunity to centralize within the same tool you’re using to manage your projects, manage your budgets.

Among other functionalities, Budgety for Monday.com offers, counts the ability to associate your budgets with your projects of interest, and even forecast the possible outcome of these budgets you’re managing. Also, it’s possible to track the app’s progress through real-time reports.

Why does this matter?

As project management success isn’t just about delivering on time, but also about doing it effectively and staying on budget. Whether you’re in marketing, product, or operations, managing project finances shouldn’t require switching tools or digging through spreadsheets. Kill those silos now!

With Budgety for Monday.com, you can:
Add and classify costs as CAPEX, OPEX, direct, or indirect
Compare expected vs. actual spending
Associate costs to tasks or boards
Track budget status in real time
Visualize data in simple reports for better decisions
Assign multiple owners to costs and expenses
Update and forecast spending without spreadsheets

Working with Budgety is simple and intuitive (if you think otherwise, please let us know!), and it’s built to help teams of all sizes make better, faster, more confident decisions

If you’ve ever wished for a way to bring financial context directly to Monday.com, now’s the time.

If you have questions, comments, feedback, or suggestions, as always, please comment or contact us. We’re here to help and keep building alongside this really cool community.

Try Budgety now
